message: fix(repair): preserve metadata loss on clamp

 {"id":"bd-w8amw","title":"Freeze exact TxnId and CommitSeq debug contracts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T04:51:31.106341415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T04:56:37.203547933Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T04:56:37.201452016Z","close_reason":"Froze exact TxnId and CommitSeq debug output; verified with focused rch tests plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wch7b","title":"add mounted-path btrfs user.xattr XATTR_CREATE/XATTR_REPLACE errno coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path xattr coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` currently proves basic set/get/list/remove semantics, but not the operator-visible `XATTR_CREATE` / `XATTR_REPLACE` flag contract that `ffs-fuse` and `OpenFs::btrfs_setxattr` already support. This leaves create-vs-replace errno parity unproven on the public mount surface.

Scope:
- Reuse or extend the mounted-path xattr helper to issue flagged `setxattr` calls on a real btrfs FUSE mount.
- Assert `XATTR_CREATE` rejects existing names with `EEXIST` while preserving the original value.
- Assert `XATTR_REPLACE` rejects missing names with the stable missing-xattr errno (`ENODATA`/`ENOATTR`) and does not create the attribute as a side effect.
- Cover at least one regular-file path and keep the assertions independent of ext4-specific behavior.

Acceptance:
- btrfs mounted-path tests prove create/replace errno semantics and no-side-effect guarantees.
- Any transport-level skips are explicit and distinguishable from backend regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:08.677704654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.040647817Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T10:59:33.038372449Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wdfbc","title":"Add metamorphic coverage for htree insert-order invariance and lookup equivalence","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:47:37.536119444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:54:11.718521091Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:54:11.715209959Z","close_reason":"Added metamorphic coverage proving htree insertion over unique hashes is order-invariant and preserves lookup equivalence across queries; verified with rch cargo test -p ffs-dir proptest_htree_insert_order_invariance_for_unique_hashes -- --nocapture, rch cargo check --workspace --all-targets, rch cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-wehdi","title":"Preserve metadata loss cost when adaptive repair symbols are clamped","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:13:14.092668545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:24:21.541897310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:24:21.539839293Z","close_reason":"Fixed metadata expected-loss recomputation after adaptive repair symbol clamping; added autopilot and pipeline regression tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","ffs-repair"]}
 {"id":"bd-weoal","title":"add btrfs BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO ioctl support","description":"Survey finding: BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO is a fundamental read-only btrfs ioctl that returns filesystem metadata (max_id, num_devices, fsid, nodesize, sectorsize, clone_alignment). Not currently implemented.

Scope:
- Add FsOps::get_btrfs_fs_info() trait method with default EOPNOTSUPP for non-btrfs
- Implement btrfs path extracting fields from superblock/context
- Define BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO constant (0x8400941F) and struct btrfs_ioctl_fs_info_args layout
- Wire dispatch in ffs-fuse ioctl handler
- Add unit dispatch tests
- Add mounted-path E2E test using btrfs-progs fi show or direct ioctl

Acceptance:
- BTRFS_IOC_FS_INFO returns correct metadata for btrfs images
- Direct dispatch tests verify struct encoding
- ext4 images return EOPNOTSUPP cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:05.113879951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:26:06.461752819Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:26:06.459362315Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-wikzk","title":"ffs-repair clippy failure: redundant closure in self_healing_demo_e2e.rs","description":"In crates/ffs-repair/tests/self_healing_demo_e2e.rs, clippy reports a redundant closure at line 65: .take_while(|c| c.is_ascii_digit()). It should be simplified to use the method directly: .take_while(char::is_ascii_digit).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:08:12.589855380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:21:54.271302235Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:21:54.268777016Z","close_reason":"Verified redundant-closure fix already present in self_healing_demo_e2e.rs at take_while(char::is_ascii_digit); rch clippy -p ffs-repair --all-targets -- -D warnings passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
